name: linfa (Rust ML toolkit)

performance.notes: Rust-native performance; ndarray backend; no Python GIL overhead

agent_readiness.notes: Rust equivalent of scikit-learn. Classical ML algorithms: linear regression, logistic regression, SVM, k-means, DBSCAN, Gaussian mixture models, decision trees, random forests, naive Bayes, PCA, kernel methods. Trait-based API (Fit, Predict). ndarray arrays as data format. Companion crates for each algorithm (linfa-linear, linfa-clustering, linfa-svm, etc.). Suitable for Rust ML pipelines needing classical ML without Python.

gotchas: Less mature than scikit-learn — not all algorithms implemented
Rust compile times add to development cycle
Smaller ecosystem and community than Python ML
Some algorithms have fewer hyperparameter options
best_when: Classical ML algorithms in production Rust services
Avoiding Python FFI/subprocess for ML inference
Rust webservices needing embedded ML (no Python runtime)
avoid_when: When Python ML ecosystem availability matters
summary: linfa is a Rust ML toolkit — the Rust equivalent of scikit-learn. Provides classical ML algorithms (linear/logistic regression, SVM, k-means, PCA, random forests) via a trait-based Fit/Predict API using ndarray. Best for production Rust services that need embedded classical ML without Python runtime overhead. Less mature than scikit-learn but growing rapidly.
